call_to_action.start: Sovereignty is increasingly important. Cella wants to partner with cloud & service providers that are open source and/or European.
compare_intro: Have a look at how Cella compares to existing sync solutions such as Electric, Zero, PowerSync and more ..
cards.blocknote_text: Block-based rich-text editor with realtime collaboration options and a minimal setup
cards.brevo_text: Brevo sends transactional emails and more.
cards.gleap_text: Gleap is an all-in-one customer feedback tool. Live chat, customer satisfaction, public roadmap and more.
cards.matrix_text: Matrix is an open network for secure, decentralized communication. Real-time chat, VoIP and more.
cards.onedollarstats_text: Affordable web analytics. Track event effortlessly. Access insights through dashboards or mobile app.
cards.paddle_text: Complete billing solution: Accept payments, handle taxes and manage subscriptions.
cards.scaleway_text: Scaleway offers compute, object storage, managed postgres, generative API.
cards.transloadit_text: Transloadit is a file handling service that helps you with uploading, transforming and storing files.
stack.drizzle_text: Postgres DB with Drizzle ORM. Seeds and easy migrations.
stack.hono_text: A simple and fast node server, easily extended with middleware.
stack.openapi_text: Autogenerated types, zod schemas, client SDK & API docs.
stack.react_text: React with Tanstack Router. Tanstack Query for fetching and caching.
stack.baseui_text: Accessible components with BaseUI, Tailwind and Motion.
stack.vite_text: A straightforward and fast monorepo setup. Vite PWA included.
stack.yjs_text: Realtime collaboration with presence as part of a rich-text editor.
stack.pulumi_text: Infrastructure as code for repeatable setup. Deploy using GitHub Actions.
stack.artillery_text: Load testing for critical flows has been made easy.
cella_approach: Since these features are complex, sync frameworks and sync databases offer to help you. But they come with their own data/query layers. So it's either all or nothing.
cella_approach2: Cella has a different approach: Postgres, API & React Query are core primitives for all data. Some data is synced, some isn't. All data flows through your API.
hero.text: Cella is an open-source TypeScript template to build modern web apps. Its sync engine works with a classic REST API, not around it.
hero.title: Adding sync & offline features should be boring.
how_it_works.text: Sync frameworks are hard to adopt and even harder to stay aligned with as your app evolves. Cella sidesteps that: a template you - and your coding agents - can read, adapt, and own from day one.
pricing.plan_1.title: pay €1k
pricing.plan_2.title: receive €1-12k
pricing.plan_3.title: To be decided
pricing.text_1: 50% to sponsor OS libraries and 50% to a Cella bounty program.
pricing.text_2: Earn while building your own product. Open source or 'community source' parts of your code.
pricing.text_3: Do you have a product that integrates well with CellaJS? Become a partner.
showcase.text: Don't just take our word for it — 🧐 explore what we're building with Cella.
showcase.text_1: An issue tracker to manage multiple app projects from a single page view. Built for speed & fun.
sync_flow: Cella is based on the notify-then-fetch pattern: a Change Data Capture worker receives Postgres changes and sends updates to API by WebSocket → API sends an SSE notification to client → (React Query) client then fetches (cached) data.
text_2: Receiving data in real time, continuing while offline, instant UI update when searching or creating a post. Users expect these features in modern web apps.
text_3: Best-in-class TypeScript libraries. And they play nice together.
features_subtext: For a complete list of features, <featuresLink>click here</featuresLink>.
text_5: Cella is open source and wants to work together with other OS projects & teams.
text_6: How to build a dedicated & sustainable community around CellaJS? Membership perhaps.
text_7: Frequently asked questions. Is your question not included? Please reach out to us.
why.text_3: Building secure, compliant and scalable web apps on European infra is still hard. Cella makes it easier.
why.text_2: Authentication, organizations, users. An entity hierarchy model with permission manager. And much more.
why.text_1: Postgres, OpenAPI & React Query are core primitives for all data flows. Some data is synced, some is not.
features.entity_hierarchy_text: One createEntityHierarchy() builder defines your whole taxonomy — user, context and product entities — and that single config drives RLS, permissions, menus and counters. Add a 'project' or 'book' in one place.
features.context_entities_text: Entities with memberships and roles. A cache enrichment pipeline auto-attaches each user's membership, a computed permission map and ancestor slugs — without extra API calls.
features.product_entities_text: Your daily-use content models. Start as plain REST, then opt into transaction tracking, an offline queue and a realtime stream — progressively, never all-or-nothing.
features.schema_evolution_text: Ship schema changes without breaking older clients. Additive migrations plus lens adapters translate between wire-format versions on the fly.
features.user_text: A complete user entity — profile, settings, sessions, passkeys and OAuth identities — wired into authentication out of the box.
features.organization_text: The built-in context entity with memberships, invitations and a configurable role hierarchy you can reshape per app.
features.attachment_text: A synced product entity in action: resumable Transloadit uploads, signed URLs and per-organisation realtime updates.
features.page_text: A public product entity with realtime Yjs co-editing, streamed over an unauthenticated SSE channel — no login required.
features.notify_then_fetch_text: A CDC worker tails the Postgres WAL and pushes a tiny SSE notification; React Query then fetches the changed rows. No polling, no fat payloads on the wire.
features.offline_queue_text: Edits made offline persist to IndexedDB, squash per field and replay on reconnect — after pulling upstream first, so conflicts stay rare.
features.gap_detection_text: Per-entity sequence numbers tell you exactly what changed while you were away; catchup delta-fetches only that seq range and removes soft-deleted rows.
features.per_field_merge_text: No 409s. Scalars resolve by HLC last-write-wins, sets merge as conflict-free add-wins deltas, and descriptions merge character-by-character with Yjs.
features.multi_tab_text: One leader tab owns the SSE connection via Web Locks and broadcasts to followers over BroadcastChannel — every tab can edit, none double-connects.
features.public_private_text: An authenticated app stream for your org data and an unauthenticated public stream for shareable content — each with its own cursor and scope.
features.rls_text: Postgres RLS on every product entity table, fail-closed: missing tenant context returns zero rows. A safety net beneath the guard chain, not a replacement for it.
features.guard_chain_text: Defense in depth on every request: authGuard → tenantGuard → orgGuard validate session, tenant and membership before any handler runs.
features.tenant_isolation_text: A hard tenant boundary enforced in the database — composite foreign keys block mismatched 'franken-rows' and RLS blocks cross-tenant reads.
features.permission_manager_text: Hierarchy-aware RBAC with a Zanzibar-style 'own' relation — resolves allow / deny / own by walking memberships from the most specific context up to the root.
features.immutability_text: BEFORE UPDATE triggers freeze identity columns (tenant_id, organization_id, user_id) after creation, and the activity log stays fully append-only.
features.magic_link_text: Passwordless sign-in by default — a single-use tokenised link sent by email, rate-limited and brute-force protected.
features.passkey_text: WebAuthn passkeys in a dedicated table: phishing-resistant primary auth with no passwords to leak.
features.oauth_text: Sign in with GitHub, Google or Microsoft via the arctic library, with PKCE on Google and Microsoft.
features.totp_text: Time-based one-time passwords as an MFA fallback, available after a passkey primary auth.
features.impersonation_text: Debug and support as any user with sysadmin impersonation — gated behind an IP allowlist and typed impersonation sessions.
features.responsive_text: Mobile-first layouts with breakpoint-aware components that adapt cleanly from phone to desktop.
features.pwa_text: An installable PWA with a Workbox service worker that precaches assets for true offline use — test it locally with `pnpm offline`.
features.i18n_text: Namespaced, snake_case translation keys with runtime locale switching — English and Dutch included.
features.data_grid_text: A React data grid with sorting, multi-selection and column management for content-heavy views.
features.blocknote_editor_text: A block-based BlockNote editor with realtime collaboration and presence, powered by a standalone Yjs relay worker.
features.resizable_panels_text: Resizable, collapsible kanban-style panels that remember their sizing across sessions.
features.drawer_patterns_text: Sheets, dialogs and dropdowns become drawers on mobile automatically — one component, two form factors.
features.storybook_text: Storybook stories to build and review components in isolation.
features.openapi_sdk_text: An OpenAPI-first backend generates a fully typed SDK — zod schemas, types and a fetch client — with a single `pnpm sdk`.
features.file_based_routing_text: Drop a file in routes/ and TanStack Router registers it into a generated, type-safe route tree — no manual wiring.
features.cella_cli_text: Stay in sync with upstream Cella — the CLI pulls bug fixes and new features into your fork with minimal merge friction.
features.drizzle_orm_text: Schema-first, type-safe Drizzle ORM — change a schema and generate the migration with `pnpm generate`.
features.modular_structure_text: A flat-root pnpm monorepo where every feature lives in its own module — scaffold only what you need and pull upstream with less friction.
features.pulumi_iac_text: Repeatable infrastructure as code on Scaleway, driven by the infra CLI — deploy with `pnpm infra`.
features.docker_text: Auto-generated Docker Compose for local dev and a multi-stage Dockerfile for lean production images.
features.eu_cloud_text: EU-sovereign deployment on Scaleway for data residency and compliance from day one.
features.github_actions_text: Push to main and CI ships it — zero-downtime rolling deploys on every commit.
features.zero_downtime_text: Rolling deployments gated by health checks, with automatic rollback when a release misbehaves.
features.otel_text: End-to-end OpenTelemetry traces, metrics and structured logs across backend, CDC, Yjs and frontend.
features.load_testing_text: Artillery load tests with deterministic seeds and saved baselines — just run `pnpm bench`.
features.ttl_cache_text: A server-side TTL entity cache with singleflight coalescing turns one DB query into fan-out for every subscriber.
features.cdc_batching_text: A TransactionBuffer groups WAL events per database transaction, collapsing N row changes into one SSE notification per type and context.
